LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETER GRIFFITH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED. DEFENDANT MAINTAINS HIS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN AND PLAINTIFF WILLIAM CARLILE FAILS TO PRESENT ANY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE. THE ONLY EVIDENCE MR. CARLILE PRESENTED IS HIS DECLARATION STATING THAT MR. GRIFFITH PAID HIM FROM PERSONAL ACCOUNTS AT VARIOUS TIMES, MR. CARLILE WORKED FROM MR. GRIFFITH'S HOME, AND MR. GRIFFITH ASKED HIM TO PERFORM TASKS INVOLVING MR. GRIFFITH'S PERSONAL FINANCES. THESE FACTS ARE NOT SUFFICIENT TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER AN EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP EXISTED BETWEEN MR. GRIFFITH AND MR. CARLILE APART FROM THE ALLEGED EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN MR. CARLILE AND THE OTHER DEFENDANTS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH BOTH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANTS ALCHEMY NETWORKS, LLC, PETULANT CHILD, LLC, AND PETER GRIFFITH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FIRST SEVEN CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. THE EIGHTH CLAIM FOR VIOLATION OF PAGA IS A REPRESENTATIVE CLAIM AND, ALTHOUGH THE PARTIES DID NOT DISCUSS THE ISSUE AND THE COURT DID NOT FIND ANY AUTHORITY ON IT, THE COURT FINDS THAT A SELF-REPRESENTED PLAINTIFF, AT LEAST ONE WHO IS NOT ADMITTED TO THE CALIFORNIA BAR, IS NOT PERMITTED TO ALLEGE A PAGA CLAIM, WHICH CAN HAVE A BINDING EFFECT ON THE RIGHTS OF PARTIES OTHER THAN THE PLAINTIFF. IN THE EVENT THAT AN ATTORNEY SUBSTITUTES IN AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF, PLAINTIFF MAY SEEK LEAVE TO ALLEGE A PAGA CLAIM. DEFENDANTS SHALL FILE THEIR ANSWER WITHIN 20 DAYS. SINGLE ORDER AS TO BOTH DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Michael Welch, Esq. (415.399.1500) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Gordon J. Zuiderweg of the Law Offices of Barry K. Rothman (310.557.0062) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PETER GRIFFITH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED. Defendant maintains his initial summary judgment burden and plaintiff William Carlile fails to present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue. The only evidence Mr. Carlile presented is his declaration stating that Mr. Griffith paid him from personal accounts at various times, Mr. Carlile worked from Mr. Griffith's home, and Mr. Griffith asked him to perform tasks involving Mr. Griffith's personal finances. These facts are not sufficient to create a triable issue whether an employment relationship existed between Mr. Griffith and Mr. Carlile apart from the alleged employment relationship between Mr. Carlile and the other defendants. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with both CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff WILLIAM CARLILE, Tel No. 415-407-3452 appearing in pro per;
Atty. GORDON ZUIDERWEG, ESQ. of Law Offices of Barry K. Rothman, Tel No. 310-557-0062 appearing telephonically for Defendants Alchemy Networks, LLC, Petulant Child, and Peter Griffith.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS ALCHEMY NETWORKS, LLC, PETULANT CHILD, LLC, AND PETER GRIFFITH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, defendants' demurrer is overruled as to the first seven causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action. The first seven claims are adequately alleged. The eighth claim for violation of PAGA is a representative claim and, although the parties did not discuss the issue and the court did not find any authority on it, the court finds that a self-represented plaintiff, at least one who is not admitted to the California Bar, is not permitted to allege a PAGA claim, which can have a binding effect on the rights of parties other than the plaintiff. In the event that an attorney substitutes in as counsel for plaintiff, plaintiff may seek leave to allege a PAGA claim. Defendants shall file their answer within 20 days. SINGLE ORDER AS TO BOTH DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Motion To Set Aside Defaults And Default Judgments
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. GORDON ZUIDERWEG, ESQ. of Law Offices of Barry K. Rothman, Tel No. 310-557-0062 appearing telephonically for Defendants Alchemy Networks, LLC, Petulant Child, LLC, and Peter Griffith;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALCHEMY NETWORKS, LLC, PETULANT CHILD, LLC AND PETER GRIFFITH'S Motion To Set Aside Defaults And Default Judgments is granted on the condition that they pay $631.13 to plaintiff within 20 days. Defendants have shown the required mistake, inadvertence and/or excusable neglect entitling them to set aside the defaults. Moreover, the strong policy favoring adjudication on the merits favors granting the motion. Plaintiff should not be out of pocket due to defendants' error and thus should be entitled to his costs and a reasonable value for the time he spent. The court credits paragraph 5 of plaintiff's declaration that plaintiff called defendants' counsel prior to seeking entry of the defaults, but defendants' counsel improperly cut short that conversation, which made it reasonable for plaintiff to seek entry of defendants' defaults. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)
